result:
Implemented first-class note attachments across the note model, CLI, and web UI.

In [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go), notes now parse and persist `Attachment:` metadata, store uploaded/copied files under `notes/.attachments/<note-id>/`, and render Markdown image embeds to served attachment URLs. I added a new `attach` CLI command that copies one or more files into a note and optionally appends image embeds with `--embed`. Rename now moves attachment directories with the note.

The web UI now serves attachments, shows an attachments section on note pages, previews images, and supports multipart uploads from the note view with an option to append Markdown image embeds. Edit pages also surface existing attachments. I updated the note renderer so standard Markdown images like `![alt](.attachments/<note-id>/<file>)` display correctly in the browser.

I also updated [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md) with the new command and note format, and added attachment-focused tests in [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go) for parsing, CLI attach behavior, image rendering, upload handling, and attachment serving.

I could not run `gofmt` or `go test` here because this environment does not have the Go toolchain installed (`go` and `gofmt` are missing from the filesystem), so that validation step is still outstanding.
